_comment: Girl Beer internal (non-client-billable) make-good demos at H-E-B locations, 2026-07-04. Parsed from HEB_Raw_Recap_Data.xlsx. Reserved keys 'Date' + 'Store/Location' become the event date + address; every other key maps to a recap template field by name. BA credited as external_ba_name.
engagements_field: Number of Customers Engaged (talked to or sampled product)

rows.What flavors were available to taste?: Pineapple Yuzu, Grapefruit Guava, Strawberry Watermelon, Peach
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 2: How much sugar and how many calories are in it?
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 3: Which flavor is the least sweet?
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 4: Can I buy the flavors separately?
rows.Positive Feedback From Customers: Consumers said Pineapple Yuzu was light and refreshing. Several liked that it was not overly sweet and said the cans would be good for a pool day. One shopper said the Pineapple flavor was their favorite. Another said it would be good for a day at the lake.
rows.Negative Feedback / Concerns From Custom: A few shoppers said the Girl Beer name felt gender-specific. One consumer wanted a stronger fruit flavor. Another expected the product to taste sweeter based on the packaging.
rows.How was the setup?: Set up near the beer aisle with chilled cans and visible cases. Traffic was steady and the table was easy to approach.
rows.Did the demo influence the store to plac: Maybe. The associate said sales would be reviewed after the weekend.
rows.Anything that could make future demos be: Use larger branded signage and sample both variety packs if inventory allows.

rows.Store/Location: 701 S Capital of Texas Hwy Bldg C, West Lake Hills, TX 78746
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 1: Is this a beer or a hard seltzer?
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 3: Do you carry the Purple variety pack?
rows.Positive Feedback From Customers: Customers liked the smooth, light finish and colorful packaging. One shopper said it would be easy to drink outdoors in the summer. Another said the cans were cute and would stand out at a cookout.
rows.Negative Feedback / Concerns From Custom: Some shoppers felt the price was a little high. Two consumers wanted more pronounced fruit flavor. One person asked for single-flavor packs instead of a variety pack.
rows.How was the setup?: The setup was clean but visibility from the main aisle was limited. Product was stocked a short distance from the table.
rows.Did the demo influence the store to plac: Unsure. The beer manager was not available at recap.
rows.Anything that could make future demos be: Request a higher-traffic placement and confirm Purple and Red variety-pack inventory before arrival.

rows.What flavors were available to taste?: Purple Variety Pack, Red Variety Pack, Pineapple Yuzu, Grapefruit Guava
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 2: How many calories are in each can?
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 4: Do other H-E-B stores carry it?
rows.Positive Feedback From Customers: Consumers described the beer as refreshing and fruit-forward. The package design attracted attention and encouraged questions. One shopper said it tasted more like beer than seltzer. Another said the packaging stood out on the shelf.
rows.Negative Feedback / Concerns From Custom: One shopper thought the alcohol percentage was too low. A few said the flavor was subtler than expected. Another consumer said they would prefer a higher ABV.
rows.How was the setup?: Product was cold and visible beside the table. The beer aisle had moderate traffic with several repeat walk-bys.
rows.Did the demo influence the store to plac: Maybe. Inventory moved during the demo, but the beer manager was unavailable.
rows.Anything that could make future demos be: Add a flavor guide and keep extra cold product ready so the table does not run out of samples.

rows.What flavors were available to taste?: Blueberry Lavender, Pineapple Yuzu, Strawberry Watermelon, Peach
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 4: Why is it called Girl Beer?
rows.Positive Feedback From Customers: Shoppers said the beer was crisp, light, and easy to drink. Blueberry Lavender was described as different but pleasantly balanced. One person said the blueberry flavor was unusual in a good way. Another called it easy to drink.
rows.Negative Feedback / Concerns From Custom: A few people preferred a stronger beer flavor. One consumer was not interested because of the product name. Another wanted the option to purchase only one flavor.
rows.How was the setup?: The table was positioned near the beer cooler with clear access and enough space for shoppers to stop without blocking the aisle.
rows.Did the demo influence the store to plac: Maybe. The store still had inventory on hand after the demo.
rows.Anything that could make future demos be: Display calorie, ingredient, and ABV information on the table to answer common questions faster.

rows.Store/Location: 2800 E Whitestone Blvd, Cedar Park, TX 78613
rows.What flavors were available to taste?: Purple Variety Pack, Red Variety Pack, Pineapple Yuzu
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 2: How much does a variety pack cost?
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 4: Which aisle is it stocked in?
rows.Positive Feedback From Customers: Customers liked that it was light and easy-drinking. Several said the smaller fruit notes made it feel more like beer than seltzer. One shopper liked that it was not sugary. Another said it was lighter than expected in a good way.
rows.Negative Feedback / Concerns From Custom: Traffic near the sampling area was slow. One consumer said the beer was too light for their preference. Another wanted more fruit flavor.
rows.How was the setup?: The setup was neat and close to inventory, but the section had lower traffic than the main beverage aisle.
rows.Did the demo influence the store to plac: Unsure. The store had enough product remaining after the demo.
rows.Anything that could make future demos be: Move closer to a cross-aisle or endcap and confirm the full flavor assortment before starting.

rows.Store/Location: 100 Carlos G Parker Blvd NW #101, Taylor, TX 76574
rows.What flavors were available to taste?: Purple Variety Pack, Red Variety Pack, Pineapple Yuzu, Tangerine
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 1: How much sugar is in it?
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 3: Is this good for summer events?
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 4: Would I like this if I do not normally drink beer?
rows.Positive Feedback From Customers: Consumers called the beer light, crisp, and not overly sweet. Tangerine received positive feedback from shoppers who wanted a brighter flavor. One shopper said Tangerine was refreshing. Another said it would be good by the pool.
rows.Negative Feedback / Concerns From Custom: A few customers questioned the name. One shopper said they normally prefer a heavier beer. Another thought the name might keep some people from trying it.
rows.How was the setup?: The setup was easy and the product was visible from the aisle. Branded signage helped shoppers understand the flavor options.
rows.Did the demo influence the store to plac: Maybe. The associate said variety packs were moving, but no order was placed during the demo.
rows.Anything that could make future demos be: Bring an additional flavor sign and position full cases next to the table for easier purchase conversion.

rows.What flavors were available to taste?: Purple Variety Pack, Red Variety Pack, Pineapple Yuzu, Grapefruit Guava, Strawberry Watermelon
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 2: Which flavor is the most popular?
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 3: Is this sweeter than a regular beer?
rows.Most Common Question / Comment 4: Where else can I buy it?
rows.Positive Feedback From Customers: Customers liked the smooth taste and said the beer was refreshing without being too sweet. Store staff were helpful with product location. One shopper called it smooth and easy to finish. Another liked the mix in the Red variety pack.
rows.Negative Feedback / Concerns From Custom: Some shoppers wanted single-flavor packs. One customer said the packaging made them expect a sweeter drink. Another wanted to choose just one flavor.
rows.How was the setup?: The beer section had low pass-by traffic, but nearly everyone who stopped engaged with the ambassador and tried a sample.
rows.Did the demo influence the store to plac: Yes. The associate indicated the shelf would likely need replenishment.
rows.Anything that could make future demos be: Use directional signage from the main aisle and confirm enough cold inventory for the full demo period.
